program_en: B.Eng. in Materials Science and Engineering & X Double Major Programme
program_chi: 材料科學與工程學及X工程學士雙主修課程
learning_outcomes:
1. Major
Programme:
Upon
completion of the programme, students will be able to:
(i)  |  explain the
scientific principles and methods underlying the study and application of
traditional and modern materials (K);
(ii)  |  demonstrate an understanding
across a wide range of materials-related topics in chemistry, physics,
electronic engineering, mechanical engineering, energy and environment (K);
(iii)  |  apply knowledge
of natural sciences, engineering, and computing appropriate to develop
solutions to complex problems related to materials (K/S);
(iv)  |  design
innovative and sustainable materials-related technology solutions to meet
identified needs with appropriate economic, environmental, social, political,
ethical, cultural, health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon,
manufacturability considerations as required (S/V);
(v)  |  conduct
investigations of complex scientific and engineering problems using
research-based knowledge, design of experiments, analysis and interpretation
of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions (K/S);
(vi)  |  analyze and
evaluate the impact of engineering solutions in a global, economic,
environmental and societal context, especially the importance of health,
safety, legal, and sustainable considerations (S/V);
(vii)  |  apply ethical
principles, commit to professional ethics and norms of engineering practice
and adhere to relevant national and international laws with the understanding
of the need for diversity and inclusion (K/V);
(viii)  |  communicate
effectively and inclusively on scientific and engineering activities, such as
being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design
documentation, make effective presentations (S/V); and
(ix)  |  stay abreast of
contemporary issues and recognize the need for independent and life-long
learning, adaptability to new and emerging technologies, and critical
thinking in the context of technological change (K/S).
K = Knowledge
outcomes S = Skills
outcomes V = Values and
attitude outcomes

Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 90 units of courses. Students will take the Materials Science
and Engineering offered by CUHK as the 1st Major, and choose
from one of the Majors offered by CUHK or CUHK(SZ) as specified in 3.

the list of substitute/equivalent/alternative courses for details.

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  Students enrolled in the Double Major
Programme should normally spend four regular terms at CUHK(SZ) during the
four years of study, except otherwise specified in 2. below. It applies
to all students, including those who select a 2nd Major
offered by CUHK.
2.  |  Students who join term-time exchange
programme have to spend at least three regular terms at CUHK(SZ) during
the four years of study.
3.  |  Students enrolled in the Double Major
programme can take substitute/equivalent/alternative courses either at
CUHK or CUHK(SZ). The list of substitute/equivalent/alternative courses
is available at this link (https://mase.erg.cuhk.edu.hk/programme-information). Students should not take a course and its
substitute/equivalent/alternative course. Should they do so, only one
course will be counted towards the fulfillment of units requirements.
Grades of relevant substitute/equivalent/alternative courses taken at
CUHK(SZ) will be transferred to CUHK for the purpose of calculation of
GPA, Major GPA and credit units.
4.  |  Students normally have to declare their
2nd Major by the end of their first year of study.
5.  |  All courses at 2000 and above level and
courses labeled as # in the requirements of the 1st Major
Programme, courses labelled as # in the requirements of the 2nd Major, as well as 3 or 4 units of research component course offered by
the 2nd Major, will be included in the calculation of Major
GPA for honours classification.
6.  |  Courses taken to fulfill the
requirements of the 1st Major cannot be counted towards the
fulfillment of requirements of the 2nd Major, and vice versa.
7.  |  Under very special circumstances and
subject to approval by the Major programmes concerned, students may opt
out of the Double Major Programme and register for the Materials Science
and Engineering Programme offered by CUHK as single major. The decision to
opt out is irrevocable. Students who have opted out are required to
complete and fulfill all graduation requirements by following the study
scheme of the Materials Science and Engineering Programme as a single
major and spend the remaining course of study at CUHK. Students
should be aware that such decisions may result in a delay in graduation
because the requirements of the single major study scheme differ from
those of the Double Major Programme.
[a]  |  Students taking Physics or Chemistry
offered by CUHK as 2nd Major should take MATH1010.
[b]  |  Students taking Physics or Chemistry
offered by CUHK as 2nd Major should take PHYS1111.

[a]  |  Students may apply to study in the Co-operative
Education Programme (Co-op@CUHK) (5 units) in Year 2 or above. Students who
study the Co-op programme will take COOP4001, which is a 1-unit course to
be taken in each of the Summer Session of Year 3 and Term 1 of Year 4. They
will undertake a full-time placement in two specific work terms, during
which they are not allowed to register for any other courses except the
1-unit continuous assessment course COOP4001. For
students who have to take more than 1 unit of course in the work term, they
should provide strong justifications for each additional course for
approval by the Co-operative Education Programme Committee and the major
programme.
